projectDescription: Embedding-space analysis of VH antibody diversity across human, mouse, and rat using ESM2 and AntiBERTy protein language models, with paired bootstrap statistics to evaluate pooling strategies and CDR masking effects.
keyFeatures: ESM2 embeddings with three pooling strategies: CLS token, full-sequence mean-pool, and CDR-only mean-pool
AntiBERTy antibody-specific embeddings with full-sequence and CDR-only mean-pool
Paired bootstrap inference (1,000 resamples) for rigorous confidence intervals on silhouette scores
UMAP 2D visualization of embedding space colored by species, V-gene family, and CDR3 length
Permutation test for null hypothesis testing of species separation
Dual bootstrap control comparing species vs V-gene family separation
Cross-model comparison: AntiBERTy vs ESM2-8M performance
Streaming data pipeline from OAS (Observed Antibody Space) with local CSV cache
publicApiSurface: src/embed.py: get_embeddings(sequences, batch_size, model_bundle, model_name) -> np.ndarray
src/embed.py: get_cdr_embeddings(sequences, cdr_indices_list, batch_size, model_bundle, model_name) -> np.ndarray
src/embed.py: precompute_esm2(sequences, cdr_indices_list, batch_size) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
src/stats.py: paired_bootstrap(Xs, labels, n, seed) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]
src/stats.py: permutation_test(X, labels, n, seed) -> tuple[float, float]
src/stats.py: dual_bootstrap(Xs, species_labels, vfam_mask, vfam_labels, n, seed) -> dict
